Here are some t
content:
Last week, we launched Gemini 3.1 TTS, our latest and best text-to-speech model. This new model introduces [awe] audio tags, an intuitive way to guide vocal style, pace, and delivery.

Here are some tips on the best ways to use audio tags in your prompts:

1. All inline tags must be enclosed in square brackets, such as [screams] or [whispers]

2. Insert these tags exactly where you want the transition to occur and make sure to avoid placing tags directly next to each other

3. Use tags like [slow] or [fast] to control the pace of the delivery, or even [short pause] or [long pause] to ramp up the anticipation in dramatic moments

4. The model also offers granular control over vocalizations, allowing you to direct the delivery with cues like [cackles] or [whispers]

5. An ideal audio tag formula could look something like: [encouraging] Let’s try that last sentence again to make sure that you nailed it. [slow] "L'oiseau s'est envolé." [short pause] Perfect! [laughs] You're a natural.

No matter what you’re developing — from [scholarly] a language learning tool, to [mysterious] an interactive podcast app, to [friendly] more adaptive customer service offerings, and beyond —  these prompting tips will equip you to start building with Gemini 3.1 TTS.

title:
GPT-5.5 takes OpenAI back to the clear number one in AI. OpenAI’s new model tops the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index by 3 points, breaking a three-way tie with Anthropic and Google

OpenAI gave
content:
GPT-5.5 takes OpenAI back to the clear number one in AI. OpenAI’s new model tops the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index by 3 points, breaking a three-way tie with Anthropic and Google

OpenAI gave us pre-release access to test all five reasoning effort levels: xhigh, high, medium, low and non-reasoning.

➤ OpenAI topping five headline evaluations: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) leads Terminal-Bench Hard, GDPval-AA and our newly hosted APEX-Agents-AA. The model trails only other OpenAI models in CritPt and AA-LCR, and comes second to Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview on three additional evaluations. The largest gains are on AA-Omniscience (+14 pts), our knowledge and hallucination benchmark, and τ²-Bench Telecom (+7 pts), a customer service agent benchmark.

➤ 20% more expensive to run our Intelligence Index: Per-token pricing has doubled from GPT-5.4 to $5/$30 per 1M input/output tokens. However, a ~40% token use reduction largely absorbs the hike - resulting in a net ~+20% cost to run our Intelligence Index.

➤ Effort a clear ladder for balancing intelligence and cost: GPT-5.5 (medium) scores the same as Claude Opus 4.7 (max) on our Intelligence Index at one quarter of the cost (~$1,200 vs $4,800) - although Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview scores the same at a cost of ~$900. GPT-5.5 (low) approximates Claude Opus 4.7 (Non-reasoning, high) on our Intelligence Index at half the cost to run (~$500 vs ~$1 ,000).

➤ Number one in GDPval-AA with an Elo of 1785: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) leads Claude Opus 4.7 (max) by ~30 pts and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview by ~470 pts. GDPval-AA is Artificial Analysis’ benchmark that leverages OpenAI’s GDPval dataset to evaluate models on real-world economically valuable tasks.

➤ Top AA-Omniscience accuracy, but trailing the frontier on hallucination: Our private AA-Omniscience benchmark rewards factual knowledge across diverse topics, but punishes hallucination. GPT-5.5 (xhigh) has the highest accuracy at 57% - meaning the model can recall facts in the Omniscience corpus more effectively than any other model. However, it has a hallucination rate of 86% - vs Opus 4.7 (max) at 36%, and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at 50%. This makes it more likely to answer a question when it does not ‘know’ the answer. The 14 pt gain in AA-Omniscience from GPT-5.4 (xhigh) was largely driven by knowledge, with a modest improvement in hallucination.

Congratulations to the team at @OpenAI and @sama on the launch

content:
1. We believe in iterative deployment; although GPT-5.5 is already a smart model, we expect rapid improvements. Iterative deployment is a big part of our safety strategy; we believe the world will be best equipped to win at the team sport of AI resilience this way.

2. We believe in democratization. We want people to be able to use lots of AI; we aim to have the most efficient models, the most efficient inference stack, and the most compute. We want our users to have access to the best technology and for everyone to have equal opportunity. We have been tracking cybersecurity as a preparedness category for a long time, and have built mitigations we believe in that enable us to make capable models broadly available.

3. We love you and we want you to win. We want to be a platform for every company, scientist, entrepreneur, and person. (My whole career has largely been about the magic of startups, and I think we are about to see that magic at hyperscale.)

content:
OpenAI just launched GPT-5.5 in ChatGPT and Codex.

It comes less than two months after OpenAI released GPT 5.4

The main shift is agentic execution: the model keeps the goal in memory, breaks work into steps, uses software, inspects what happened, and corrects course when the first pass fails.

GPT-5.5 matches GPT-5.4 on per-token latency while raising scores on Terminal-Bench 82.7%, OSWorld 78.7%, and GDPval 84.9%, while using fewer tokens on the same Codex tasks.

Currently rolling out to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users in ChatGPT and Codex.

Sam Altman argued that token efficiency gains offset the cost—GPT-5.5 completes the same Codex tasks with fewer tokens, which means cheaper runs even at a higher per-token rate.

content:
Claude broke the internet (again) with Claude Design.

Here's how it designs websites, decks & videos:

1. You need a Pro or Max plan.
2. Go to claude .ai/design (separate URL).
3. Team/Enterprise? Your admin turns it on via Settings → Capabilities → Anthropic Labs.

If you want a free guide with screenshots, go here:
↳ https://t.co/ZY8Fg5CuoC

Here are my favorite use cases:

✦ Pitch a $100 Million fundraise in one shot.

Prompt: "Create a high-fidelity landing page designed to raise $[AMOUNT] from [TARGET INVESTORS] for '[PRODUCT NAME]' - [short description]. Target audience: [audience]. Tone should feel [feeling] - think a mix of [brand] + [brand] + [ecosystem]."

✦ Turn any blog post into a pitch deck.

Prompt 1 (upload the blog as markdown): "Make a 30-second animated video that summarizes this blog for a first-time viewer."
Prompt 2 (same chat): "Now convert that video into a slide pitch deck."

The video step forces visual thinking first. The deck comes out 10x better than asking for it directly.

✦ Design like Mastercard, Airbnb, or Ferrari.

Go to getdesign .md (free). 
Download the DESIGN .md of any brand you love.
Upload it to Claude Design + your product brief.

Prompt: "Use the attached DESIGN .md. Create a [landing page / pitch deck] for [my product]."

It designs in that brand's exact style. Fonts, colors, tone, everything.

I made over 100+ infographics like this.

If you want to download all of them, just:
1. Go to https://t.co/psB7XxAv8w.
2. Then enter your work email.
3. It will ask you to pay or not. Don't pay.
4. Wait 2 min. Then open the welcoming email.
5. Click on my Notion library with everything in it.

Disclaimer: you will be subscribed to my newsletter. It's free, and always will be. Some people pay only to join my community & get answers faster.

500,000 readers enjoy it twice a week.

To always stay ahead of the AI curve.
To master AI, before it masters you.

♻️ Repost this to help others get better at Claude.

title:
【速報】Claude Code for Healthcare: How Physicians Build with AI が開催：基盤モデルのベンダーで初となる、医師がVibe Codingによって開発したプロダクトの紹介とデモを行うイベント

Anthropic Hackathon優勝者の医師が開発したアプリ計6個のデモとディスカッションが行われました（URLもあり）

それぞれ解説します👇
content:
【速報】Claude Code for Healthcare: How Physicians Build with AI が開催：基盤モデルのベンダーで初となる、医師がVibe Codingによって開発したプロダクトの紹介とデモを行うイベント

Anthropic Hackathon優勝者の医師が開発したアプリ計6個のデモとディスカッションが行われました（URLもあり）

それぞれ解説します👇

■ グラハム医師(救急医・MDCalc共同創設者)が作った3つのプロダクト

1. 乳児検診ノート生成ツール
アプガースコア、バイタルサイン、スクリーニング項目を入力するだけで検診記録を自動生成。地域ごとの検査項目の違い(カリフォルニア vs サウジアラビアなど)もClaude Codeに指示するだけで即座に対応。わずか3分で作成。既存の電子カルテ(EHR)のテンプレートは「全診療科共通」で使いにくいが、Claude Codeなら各診療科の専門性に合わせた小さなツールを数分で自作できる。

2. 複雑な臨床記録の要約ツール
20枚以上の看護記録や医師のメモを読み込み、視点別に要約。患者向けには専門用語を避けて「なぜ熱が出ているのか」をわかりやすく説明、専門医向けには異常バイタル・白血球数・耐性菌情報など重要データのみ抽出。医師がカルテ読み込みに費やす時間を劇的に減らし、患者と向き合う時間を増やせる。

3. 空気質・喊息管理ツール「Dude, where's my inhaler?」
喊息やCOPD患者向けに、郵便番号からリアルタイムの大気汚染データをAPIで取得。同じデータをZ世代向け(スラング混じりのアドバイス)、医師向け(高リスク患者の管理リスト)、公衆衛生当局向け(汚染スポットのヒートマップ)に加工して表示。Claude Codeが「同じデータを異なるニーズ向けに瞬時に加工できる」強みを示した。

■ ミハル医師(循環器内科医・Hackathon優勝者)が作った3つのプロダクト

4. 診察前AIアシスタント(Pre-Visit AI)
患者が診察室に入る前にチャット形式で問診。「奥さんが知っている赤い薬」のような曖昧な表現も理解し、服用薬・過去の診察歴・症状をヒアリング。内容を医学的な要約に変換し、医師が診察前に確認できる。繰り返しの質問を減らし、医師が最初から本質的な問題に集中できる。

5. MedDuties(勤務表作成・管理システム)
8年前にPHPで自作した古い勤務表システムをClaude Codeで刷新。医師の希望(休み、特定のシフト)を反映し、「ウォーカー医師にすべての夜勤を割り当てて」といった自然言語での指示にも対応。Google/iCalとの連携も。Claude Codeでたった20分で対話型チャット機能を追加。古いコードベースでも全体構造を理解してAI機能を短時間で後付けできることを証明。

6. 診察後フォローアップツール(Post-Visit AI)
診察から次の診察までの空白期間に患者をサポート。診察内容を記録し患者が自宅で確認できる他、用語解説、医師の推奨事項のチャット確認、バイタルや検査結果の追跡、医学的ガイドラインとの照合が可能。HackathonではPRDをClaudeと作成し、わずか40分でMVPを完成。開発スピードが「月単位から分単位」に変わった象徴的な例。

■ ディスカッションの要点

「医師が開発者になる」意義: エンジニアに依頼して数ヶ月待つのではなく、現場の痛みを一番知る医師自身がClaude Codeを使ってその日のうちに解決策を形にできる。ターミナルを触ったことがない医師で1週間で2つの臨床ツールを完成させた例もあり、プログラミングスキルの有無より「課題をどう定義するか」が重要になってきている。

■ HIPAA / GDPRと医療データの安全性

医療データを扱う上でプライバシー保護は最優先事項。Claude Codeを使ってPrivacy-firstな設計を迅速に行えるが、最終的には専門家による監査が必要。Anthropic側でもHIPAA準拠のためのプラグインなどを検討中。米国ではFHIR(USCDI)などの標準規格により、電子カルテ(EHR)とのAPI連携が以前より容易になっている。

医師たちが「かつてないほどクリエイティビティが解放されている」と語っていたのが印象的。

title:
A lot of very good engineers secretly feel they’re already behind on AI coding tools.

Not because they haven’t tried Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex…
but because everywhere they look, there’s some myst
content:
A lot of very good engineers secretly feel they’re already behind on AI coding tools.

Not because they haven’t tried Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex…
but because everywhere they look, there’s some mysterious combo of rules, hooks, skills and “best practices” they’re supposed to know.

So everyone does the same thing in isolation:
- Rewriting AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules in each repo
- Learning the same painful lessons on security, git and testing
- And quietly wondering if they’re “using these tools wrong”

I wanted to fix a small but real part of that.

Over the last days I pulled together a set of opinionated, battle-tested rule files for:
- Claude Code → CLAUDE.md
- Codex → AGENTS.md
- Cursor → .cursor/rules/*.mdc

They focus on the stuff that actually changes how your agents behave:
🔒 Clear security boundaries (what the agent must NEVER do)
🧠 Code style that encodes real architectural decisions, not generic lint rules
🧪 Testing philosophy (when not to add tests, and why)
🔀 Git & workflow rules that stop agents from doing surprising repo-wide edits

No threads. No PDFs.
No “comment Interested to get the link”.

The GitHub repo link is in the replies so you can just open → copy → adapt → ship.

title:
Claude Code. Gemini CLI. GitHub Copilot.

Three of the most widely used AI coding agents in the world. All compromised by the same attack a specially crafted comment in a GitHub PR.

One prompt. Arbit
content:
Claude Code. Gemini CLI. GitHub Copilot.

Three of the most widely used AI coding agents in the world. All compromised by the same attack a specially crafted comment in a GitHub PR.

One prompt. Arbitrary commands executed. Credentials extracted. Gone.

The attack success rate against current defenses: over 85%.

Here's what nobody's talking about. It wasn't just that the agents were vulnerable. It's that there was no record of what they did. No verifiable trail of what commands ran, what data was touched, what was exfiltrated.

The attack happened. But so did the silence after it.
You can patch a vulnerability. You can't patch the absence of proof.

Every AI coding agent running today is making decisions inside a black box. 

The industry is focused on building smarter agents. Nobody is focused on building accountable ones.

That's the gap. And it doesn't close itself.
